insight: Cape Verde appears to benefit from hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup, as they gained momentum in both of their breaks, resulting in a positive change in momentum of +0.100 per minute on average. Notably, both of these breaks occurred when Cape Verde was not pressing, and in these instances, they were able to maintain their momentum after the break, which could indicate that the team is effective at adjusting their strategy during these pauses.
